body:
status=open
reason=Host filesystem is out of disk space (ENOSPC). Every Bash tool invocation fails before the command runs, because the harness itself cannot mkdir its per-session scratch directory under /tmp/claude-501/... (confirmed via four independent attempts, including a fresh general-purpose subagent that hit the identical ENOSPC on its own separate mkdir). This blocks all shell execution needed for this bead: locating the land advancer's staged-changes check via grep, reading/editing cli/internal/agent source, and running `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'LandAdvancer|LandJam' -count=1`. Read/Write/Edit tools still function (this file was written without Bash), but no git or go tooling can run. This is an external host-resource blocker unrelated to the bead's code scope, not a defect in the land advancer logic. Retry once the host's /tmp (or the filesystem backing it, e.g. an OrbStack VM disk) has free space again.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes

status=open
reason=Bash tool is non-functional in this execution environment. Every invocation fails immediately with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/...'` — the harness cannot even create its per-session scratch directory under /tmp, before any actual command (git, go test, df, echo) runs. Verified directly and via an independent fresh subagent spawned in this same host — identical failure both times. This blocks all git operations (cannot inspect repo state, cannot stage/commit) and all Go test execution (cannot satisfy AC: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'LandAdvancer|LandJam' -count=1 green`). This is a host-level /tmp disk-space or inode exhaustion issue outside the worktree's control — retry once the host's /tmp has been cleared or the execution is rescheduled to a healthy host.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
rationale: status: open
reason: Bash tool is non-functional in this execution environment. Verified directly and via an independent fresh subagent spawned in this same host — identical failure both times. This blocks all git operations (cannot inspect repo state, cannot stage/commit) and all Go test execution (cannot satisfy AC: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'LandAdvancer|LandJam' -count=1 green`). This is a host-level /tmp disk-space or inode exhaustion issue outside the worktree's control — retry once the host's /tmp has been cleared or the execution is rescheduled to a healthy host.
